best_match.name: Ivy League
best_match.reason: The Ivy League cut complements a round face by adding height and structure, while being easy to maintain.

good_options.name: Crew Cut
good_options.reason: The Crew Cut is versatile and helps define the face shape.

good_options.name: Textured Crop
good_options.reason: Adds texture to straight hair, enhancing the overall vibe.

good_options.name: Side Part
good_options.reason: The Side Part adds a classic touch and works well with the face shape.

not_recommended.name: Mullet
not_recommended.reason: A Mullet may not suit a round face and could look unbalanced.

not_recommended.name: Mohawk
not_recommended.reason: A Mohawk might be too bold and does not complement a round face.

not_recommended.name: Bowl Cut
not_recommended.reason: The Bowl Cut emphasizes roundness, which may not be flattering.

analysis.parting_recommendation: A side part is recommended to add structure and contrast.
analysis.fringe_recommendation: Opt for a soft fringe to avoid emphasizing the roundness of the face.

length_options.short_verdict: Ideal for a neat, easy-to-manage look.
length_options.medium_verdict: Allows for more styling options without being too high-maintenance.
length_options.long_verdict: Not recommended as it can make the face appear rounder.

avoid.name: Golden Blonde
avoid.reason: Warm tones may clash with the Winter personal color palette.

avoid.name: Copper Red
avoid.reason: Overly warm shades can be unflattering against light skin.

analysis.summary: The Ivy League cut is the best match, offering a neat and structured look that complements the round face. A side part and soft fringe are recommended to add style without emphasizing roundness. Cool and neutral hair colors enhance the overall appearance.
care_tips: Use a lightweight styling product to maintain texture and hold.
Regular trims every 4-6 weeks to keep the style sharp.
Consider a sulfate-free shampoo to maintain natural shine.
